The Medway Cup is the Association’s annual knockout competition which has been run for many years
The current trophy, know as the Jo Jagger Cup, was introduced in 2006 in memory of one of the founding members of the Medway Basketball Association in 1947
The Medway Cup is a seeded knockout competition open to all teams in the Medway Basketball Association.
This season the Medway Cup has 17 entries See Cup details
Losing teams in the Preliminary or First Round of the Cup competition will proceed to the Plate competition
Teams defaulting in the Cup competition do NOT progress to the Plate competition

The Medway Plate is an annual knockout competition for the losing teams from the Preliminary and first Rounds of the Medway Cup competition.
The current trophy was presented to the Association in 2006 by the long term Committee member and previous Chairman of the Association Mr Brian Crouch. The trophy is known as the Nobby Crouch Plate.
Teams defaulting in the Cup competition do NOT progress to the Plate competition
This season the Medway Plate has 9 entries. See Plate details

The Division 2 PlayOff is contested by the top four teams in Division 2 at the completion of the league programme.
The PlayOffs will consist of two semi-final games for each team and the aggregate score used to determine the winners, who will then meet in a two games Final series.
Semi Finals are arranged based on the positions in the division at 3rd April 2026. 4 v 1 and 3 v 2
To be eligible to participate in a Play-Off game, a player must have played in at least 3 regular league games for that team in the current season.

The Rosebowl is an optional handicapped competition based on the teams positions in Divisions 1 & 2. The initial stage is played in groups with teams within the groups playing each other home and away. Teams heading the groups proceed to the knockout phase culminating in a final
The 2025 competition has been reduced to 6 teams opting to compete.

The score at the start of each game is the difference between the handicaps ie Team A (15) v Team B (35) – starting score 0 – 20. The winner willl be decided by the aggregate score over the two games.
Handicaps will be calculated by the Management Committee and will be based on the performances of the current season.
THE HANDICAP IS NOT OPEN TO APPEAL
Players must have played a minimum of FOUR games in MBA competitions this season prior to their first game in the Rosebowl to be eligible to take part

title: Division 1 Play In 2023/2024 – Medway Basketball Association

blocks.text: The Division 1 Play In (Bundeslega terminology) is a new competition which will help to evaluate the relative strengths of teams prior to the 2024/2025 season.

The Play In is contested by the teams finishing 5th and 6th in Division 1 along with the top two teams from Division 2 at the end of the regular league programme. The competition will use a round robin system providing each team with an additional6 games.
DIVISION 1 PLAY IN FIXTURES AND RESULTS

The Rosebowl is an optional handicapped competition based on the teams positions in Divisions 1 & 2.
The 2024 competition sees 6 teams opting to compete.
In response to paucity of available court time the competition will take on a different format this year.

Teams will be placed in two pools of three to play just two games, one at home and one away. The top two teams from each pool will proceed to a finals day comprising two semi-finals and a final to establish the winner
The score at the start of each game is the difference between the handicaps ie Team A (15) v Team B (35) – starting score 0 – 20
Handicaps will be calculated by the Management Committee and will be based on the performances of the current season.
THE HANDICAP IS NOT OPEN TO APPEAL
Players must have played a minimum of FOUR games in MBA competitions this season prior to their first game in the Rosebowl to be eligible to take part
SF2: Sevenoaks Suns v Bromley Fury
Rosebowl Final: Guru Nanak v Bromley Fury
The Supplementary League is contested by the remaining teams in Division 2 that are not competing in the Play In. Games will be organised on a round robin basis giving each team another 10 games

This season sees the extension of the PlayOff Competition to include a new Division 2 PlayOff series.
Usually the top four teams of the divisions qualify for the PlayOffs. However this year Kent Tigers are unavailable to partake in either series.

The format of each series will be A v D and B v C home and away. The winners will be decided by the aggregate score over the two games. Winners will play best of three in the finals series Court availability has limited the number of games available this season

Following the break in play due to COVID the league is a bit different this year. We are trying to get the right combination of teams for next season. The last couple of years have seen teams change significantly- some getting stronger, some having lost players and so not all teams are in the right division. This is why we are going into the Second Phase.
It is hoped to start the Second Phase as early as possible in March. The groupings will be based on the league positions after phase one – hence the need for completion ASAP.
The top FOUR teams in Div 1 will become Division A and will play each other Home and Away. This will give six more games each.

The remaining TWO teams from Div 1 will join the top THREE teams of Div 2 and become Division B. Teams will only play new opponents Home and Away so whereas the Div 2 teams will only have four more games the Div 1 teams will have six games

The remaining THREE teams from Div 2 will be joined by the top TWO teams from Div 3 and become Division C Teams will only play new opponents Home and Away so the Div 2 teams will have four more games and the DIv 3 teams will have six more games.
The remaining FOUR teams from Div 3 will become Division D and will play each other Home and Away. This will give six more games each.
For all Divisions the second phase standings will retain the results from games against the teams from the same division in phase one.
At the completion of the second phase the standings in the new divisions will inform the entry to this season’s Play-Off competition.
The final top FOUR teams in Division 1 will become Division A and will play each other Home and Away. This will give six more games each.
Results of games marked with * have been bought forward from Division 1

The continued Coronavirus pandemic led to the cancellation of all the regular competitions in the 2020/2021 season.
At the end of the season,as clubs gradually returned to play, a Summer Games competition was held under COVID protocols.
The 2019/2020 season came to an abrupt end when the country moved into lockdown due to the Coronavirus pandemic.
The league tables represent the position at the the start of the lockdown
The 2019/2020 season came to an abrupt end when the country went into lockdown in March due to the Coronavirus pandemic.
Hopes to complete some competitions during 2020 and prior to the new season were eventually dismissed and consequently those competitions were abandoned,
The 2019/2020 season came to an abrupt end when the country went into lockdown due to the Coronavirus pandemic.
In Division One, although there were only a few games to be played, Dartford Sharks had completed their programme and could not be overtaken.
Therefore the Division One winners for 2019/2020 are Dartford Sharks.
St Georges C of E School
St Georges C of E School
The 2019/2020 season came to an abrupt end when the country went into lockdown due to the Coronavirus pandemic.
In Division Two there were a large number of games that could not be completed. Oakwood Saints led the table and after due consideration using the FIBA classification system they remain as leaders.
Therefore the Division Two trophy is to be awarded to Oakwood Saints for 2019/2020

title: Division 1 Play Offs 2018/2019 – Medway Basketball Association

blocks.text: Once the top Four teams in Division 1 for 2018/2019 had been decided they proceeded to the new Play Offs competition.
1 Dartford Sharks 2 Maidstone Royals 3 Wizards 4 Bromley Fury

Teams played ‘best of three’ semi finals as follows: 1 v 4 and 2 v 3. In the event that a team won both of their first two games the third game was not required. The winners of each semi final set then played off in a best of three finals set of games.
